root_cause: BioCyc (which hosts MetaCyc) discontinued anonymous API access. All endpoints now require a free registered account with an authentication token. Every MetaCyc tool (search_pathways, get_pathway, get_compound, get_reaction) now returns an error message instructing the user to create an account at https://biocyc.org/signup.shtml.
workaround: KEGG (kegg_tools.json) covers pathways and reactions with the same scope. Reactome (reactome_tools.json) covers pathways with richer hierarchy. ChEBI (chebi_tools.json) covers small-molecule compounds. The MetaCyc-specific organism-by-organism pathway curation has no free public substitute, but the cross-organism queries that account for most MetaCyc use cases are covered by KEGG/Reactome.
investigation_notes: All four MetaCyc_* tools return the same error message — confirmed in tool source, not transient
biocyc.org public docs confirm anonymous access was removed; account is free but requires sign-up form completion
Adding API key support is a separate workstream — for now the tools are unusable as configured
